items.mistake: Treating num_leaves as interchangeable with max_depth
items.fix: For LightGBM, tune num_leaves and min_data_in_leaf; for XGBoost, tune max_depth and min_child_weight. A tree with num_leaves=64 can be deeper on one branch than max_depth=6 allows in a balanced tree.
items.why: Leaf-wise growth creates asymmetric trees — the same leaf count does not map to the same depth cap.
items.example: LightGBM num_leaves=128 with min_data_in_leaf=5 on 2k rows → severe overfit. Try num_leaves=31, min_data_in_leaf=20 first.

items.mistake: Training thousands of trees with learning_rate=0.3 and no early stopping
items.fix: Use lower learning_rate (0.01–0.1), n_estimators with early_stopping_rounds on a validation set, and rely on best_iteration.
items.why: High η plus many rounds memorizes training noise; validation loss rises while training loss keeps falling.
items.example: XGBClassifier(learning_rate=0.3, n_estimators=2000) without eval_set → train AUC 0.99, val AUC 0.72.

items.mistake: Ignoring class imbalance in binary classification
items.fix: Set scale_pos_weight (XGBoost) or class_weight / is_unbalance (LightGBM); evaluate PR-AUC and recall at business threshold, not accuracy.
items.why: Default log-loss weighting favors the majority class; the model predicts 'no fraud' everywhere and still looks accurate.

items.mistake: Assuming in-tree missing handling removes need for any preprocessing
items.fix: Compare in-tree NaN routing against explicit imputation from Missing Values on validation; watch features that are missing-not-at-random.
items.why: Learned default directions optimize loss on observed data — they do not fix informational missingness or leakage.
items.example: Income missing only for high earners who opt out — add indicator column and compare val AUC with vs without median imputation.

items.fix: Hold out validation or use CV; log metrics per boosting round (Tracking Metrics); select hyperparameters by validation score.
items.why: Boosting reduces training loss monotonically — the last trees often hurt generalization.
items.example: Log val RMSE each round; stop at iteration 187 when early_stopping fires instead of using all 1000 trees.
